content.text:
Memory stage: EntityCanonicalizer v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Stage instructions:
        You are EntityCanonicalizer v1.

Goal:
Resolve entity mentions from the latest turns into canonical entities.

Return JSON object:
{
  "operations": [
    {
      "mention": "text",
      "action": "link_existing | create_new | add_alias | noop",
      "entity_id": "uuid-or-null",
      "new_entity": {
        "entity_type": "user | person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service | environment | other",
        "canonical_name": "text",
        "summary": "text-or-null"
      },
      "about_file_assertion": false,
      "alias_text": "text-or-null",
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Prefer precision over recall.
- Do not merge distinct entities on weak evidence.
- Create new entities only when no candidate is a safe match.
- If a candidate safely matches the mention, use "link_existing" with that candidate id.
- Use "add_alias" only when the mention is a new surface form for a safe existing candidate.
- Canonical names are identity keys inside a namespace: the same canonical_name is treated as the same entity even if the entity_type differs.
- If distinct referents share the same surface form, disambiguate the canonical_name, for example "Java (programming language)" vs "Java (island)".
- If several candidates share a name, pick by entity type and summary; do not create another same-name entity just because the type differs.
- Prefer English for entity summaries; keep proper names, product names, repo names, and file names unchanged.
- Entity summaries must describe identity only, not mutable facts, current status, preferences, ownership, formats, fields, versions, or decisions. Put those facts into claims/notes instead.
- For USER first-person facts and preferences, resolve the user as the stable namespace-level USER entity named "User".
- For imported document/file_path/raw_url sources, the source path, URL, title, and section heading are source metadata. Do not create DOCUMENT, FILE, or URL-like entities merely to represent the imported source itself. Create a document/file entity only when the document content discusses that document/file as a durable domain object.
- For imported document sources, do not create FILE entities from incidental code references, stack traces, code blocks, file extensions, or paths such as "*.kt". Set action="noop" unless the text states a durable assertion about the file itself and later stages need that file as a claim subject or object.
- Set about_file_assertion=true only for FILE operations where the target text asserts something about that file itself. Set it false for non-FILE operations and incidental file-like mentions.
- Do not use raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" as entity_id. entity_id must be an existing candidate id, or null when creating a new entity.
- Use relevant retrieval context to resolve ambiguous target mentions such as pronouns, "it", "that", "first one", "second one", and "from that list".
- When TARGET_MESSAGE selects one item from a previously retrieved ordered list, create or link only the selected concrete entity so later stages can write the target claim.
- Never create chat-specific USER entities such as "User of chat:..."; a new chat is not a new user.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Detected raw mentions:
        No deterministic mention detector is available in this MVP.
Infer only concrete reusable referents from the latest turns.
For first-person USER statements, create or link a stable USER entity when claims need a subject.

        Candidate existing entities:
        - id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=Lifecycle (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Lifecycle (memory architecture axis).
- id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Note (memory representation); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Note (memory representation).
- id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Retrieval policy (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Retrieval policy (memory architecture axis).
- id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Rich note memory tier; aliases=none; summary=Concept named Rich note memory tier.
- id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Scope and lineage (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Scope and lineage (memory architecture axis).
- id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Source (memory representation); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Source (memory representation).
- id=entity:<6>; type=SERVICE; name=Memory Repair Worker; aliases=none; summary=Service named Memory Repair Worker.
- id=entity:<7>; type=CONCEPT; name=Claim (memory representation); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Claim (memory representation).

        Relevant retrieval context for ambiguous mention resolution:
        - source external:document-section:3ba1fe9fde8041a82c660a92539e97ff: Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слаба
[truncated 380 chars]
- source chat:<0> Memory ingestion source document. Treat the following as a pasted handoff architecture document, not as an immediate task.

File: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

# 01. Architecture

## 1. Цель системы

Нужна memory layer для AI-агента, которая:

- переживает границы одной сессии
- умеет хранить факты, preferences, задачи, rationale и evolving plans
- поддерживает обновление знаний, а не только накопление
- позволяет объяснить, **откуда** взялась память
- не превращает каждый turn в permanent knowledge
- умеет старить, архивировать и чинить память

## 2. Центральная архитектурная идея

В `v3` у памяти четыре оси, а не одна:

1. **Representation**
   - `Source`
   - `Claim`
   - `Note
[truncated 10644 chars]
- source external:document-section:5bc1ee88bf79494e60d9bab270290121: Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path
Document lines: 329-466

## 11. Write path

### Step 1. Persist source
Сохраняем raw input:
- text
- source type
- speaker
- timestamp
- namespace
- thread/conversation ids
- optional embedding

### Step 2. Route
Router решает:
- `noop`
- `direct_structured_write`
- `note_write`
- `mixed`

### Step 3. Retrieve candidate memories
Перед extraction достаем:
- похожие claims
- похожие notes
- open tasks
- existing profile
- candidate entities / aliases
- recent evidence

### Step 4. Canonicalize entities
Привязать mentions к existing entit
[truncated 1928 chars]
- note hot-path:note:<0> Memory v3 Write path operational sequence; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the Memory v3 write path persists raw source input, routes it to `noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, or `mixed`, retrieves candidate memories before extraction, canonicalizes entities, runs specialized extractors, reconciles candidates, applies all changes in one transaction, and schedules maintenance such as consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention/archive.
- note hot-path:note:<1> Recommended Memory v3 write and read architecture flow; The recommended architecture logs User / Tool / Document input in Source Log, routes it through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval, canonicalizes entities, dispatches to Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, reconciles claims and notes into Postgres + pgvector, runs background consolidation/repair/retention workers, and serves runtime answers through Read-Time Retrieval Planner and Answer Composer.
- note hot-path:note:<2> Memory updates must use retrieval and reconciliation, not append-only writes; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, Memory v3 write-time updates must first decide whether memory should be written, retrieve similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy such as `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, or `noop`. Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.
- note hot-path:note:<3> Truth state and retrieval state must stay separate; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, `Truth state` answers whether an item is currently valid as knowledge, while `Retrieval state` answers how it should be searched and kept in the hot path. They must not be mixed: for example, a note can be `resolved` but still `warm` for rationale retrieval, and a claim can be `superseded` but still `warm` as historical evidence.
- source external:document-section:42bd3b1e0eb1763cbd7e31e7d9ba71ff: Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура
Document lines: 49-126

## 3. Основная модель записи

Теперь у системы два semantic path и два maintenance path.

### Semantic write paths

1. **Direct structured path**
   Для вещей, которые уже можно безопасно нормализовать:
   - explicit facts
   - stable preferences
   - commitments / tasks
   - clear status changes

2. **Note path**
   Для вещей, которые уже важны, но еще не должны становиться hard facts:
   - design direction
   - rationale / trade-offs
   - evolving plans
   - lessons / patterns
   - do
[truncated 1164 chars]
- note hot-path:note:<4> Memory v3 Read path retrieval modes, budgets, and answer composition; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the Memory v3 read path first decides whether memory is needed, loads core blocks, chooses Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, or Evidence-first/evidence-fallback retrieval, enforces explicit `v3` budget caps by query type, and composes answers that distinguish verified from provisional information, account for conflicts and scope boundaries, can abstain, and do not present notes as hard facts without qualification.
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> The Memory write path requires scheduling note consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention or archive maintenance by debounce or cron.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> The recommended Memory v3 architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as its primary storage backend.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile, or episodes when the signal is strong enough.

Note maturity Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости. Полезный паттерн: - `fresh` - `stabilizing` - `mature` - `consolidated` Что может повышать maturity: - note использовался в retrieval - note подтвержден несколькими sources - note не конфликтует с новыми данными - note пережил какое-то время без supersede - note важен для recurring rationale questions Что может понижать или закрывать note: - явный supersede - устаревший scope - отсутствие использования - слабая поддержка evidence ## 14. Memory repair Repair — это отдельный background workflow. Что он чинит: - duplicate notes / claims - contradictory active items - profile drift - stale task states - bad entity merges - note spam - budget leakage в retrieval Почему это надо выделять отдельно: - consolidation делает память богаче - repair делает память здоровее Это разные работы.

Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

responseFormat.description: Resolve target message mentions into canonical memory entities.
responseFormat.name: memory_entity_canonicalizer

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.
